title: gcp-sm: project/secret/version not URL-encoded in REST paths
description: Trigger: URI like secretx://gcp-sm/my-project%23foo/my-secret (percent-decoded '#' in project name) or any project/secret/version value containing URL-special chars (#, ?, &, space) after SecretUri::parse percent-decodes them. Bad behavior: access_url() and add_version_url() interpolate these values directly into the REST path string via format\!(), creating a malformed URL. reqwest receives a URL with a fragment (#) that truncates the path, a premature query string (?), or similar corruption. The GCP API request fails with a confusing error (404 on wrong path, or HTTP client error) rather than a clear InvalidUri. Fix: percent-encode each path segment using urlencoding::encode() or similar before interpolating into the URL string.
